feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Road Map Explorer
feed.subtitle: SAP Road Map Explorer blog posts in SAP Community

entry.title: Use the SAP Road Map Explorer in 5 steps!
entry.content:
The SAP Road Map Explorer was recently released to help SAP customers customize road maps according to their needs. In 5 simple steps, customers can navigate the tool in an easy and intuitive way.

To first access the tool, log-in with your S-user credentials or you can create an account for free on roadmaps.sap.com. Then, simply follow the steps below to start exploring:

 

Step #1: Search for your topic


There are 3 ways you can view a road map: 1) Browse on the top according to a product, industry, or business processes, 2) Use the search bar to type the topic of your choice, or 3) Click on the “Most viewed Road Maps” at the bottom.

Tip: As you type on the search bar, suggestions will appear, and you can choose which one best fit your search. Currently, “free-text” functionality or typing freely on the search bar is not available so please choose one of the suggestions to yield the best results.


Landing Page


 

Step #2: Customize with filters


On top of the Kanban board, there are 5 categories you can choose from: Products, Processes, Industries, Focus Topics, and Suite Qualities. Numbers on the right side of filters indicate show many innovations will appear if you choose that filter. Filters will only appear if there are innovations available to show so you’ll never experience 0 results. The Time Horizon on the right allows you to manually select quarters from past and future years.

Tip: Choosing 2+ filters within the same category (i.e. choosing 2 products) follows the “OR” formula. Innovations with Product A or Product B will appear on the Kanban board. Choosing 2+ filters from different categories (i.e. choosing 1 product and 1 industry) follows the “AND” formula. This means the Explorer will show innovations that contain Product A and Industry B.


Filters on the Kanban board



Time Horizon Filter



Step #3: Explore innovations on the Kanban board


The Kanban board is the page that showcases all the innovations (or product features) in quarters. Use the left/right scrolls to browse through the road map. Each quarter column has arrows on the top right – click on each one to change your view preferences of the innovations (collapse or expand the innovation cards).

Click on each innovation card or box to view more details such as the innovation description and benefits. Planned innovations will have additional information such as features and the technical view.

Tip: Use the “star” button to favorite certain innovations that stand out to you! This makes it easier to spot innovations on the Kanban board.


Kanban Board



Innovation Details


 

Step #4: Share with your colleagues


The top right of each innovation card has a share button, giving you a link to copy and paste. Once you save your road map and/or innovations, “My Road Maps” on the top right of your page will also have a sharing link.


Sharing Feature


 

Step #5: Save & Download


The top right corner of the Kanban board has a “Save” button which allows you to save the full road map you created with the selected filters. Easily access them later under “My Road Maps”. If you want to save certain innovations, click on the “+” button beside the innovation name to add it to your “Innovation List”, which you can also access under “My Road Maps”.

Lastly, a download button is available beside the “Download” button, which will automatically produce an Excel file with all innovations pertaining to the road map you created.


Save & Download Feature



Innovation Set Feature


 

You can start exploring now at roadmaps.sap.com. For more information on how to use the tool and any updates, view and follow the SAP Road Maps team's Wiki Page.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Live Must-Know Interactive Training
entry.content:
Are you short on time? Do you want a training session that is packed with only the must-know facts on a topic?

We have the answer for you! New, lively, interactive Live Must-Know training. Live Must-Know sessions are highly interactive, we welcome and encourage your questions throughout, so you get the most out of every session. See what differentiates this modern take on the classic webcast format.

So what are Live Must-Know sessions, I hear you ask…..

  1. They are interactive and full of "must-know" facts

  2. They are live, often feature demo's and there is no recording

  3. You are encouraged to ask questions throughout the sessions with engineered pauses

  4. They are long enough to give you the facts but short enough to be cost effective with time ( ~100 mins)

  5. You will get the presentation slides full with useful tips and additional resources

  6. These sessions are hosted in many languages - visit the abstracts for language details

  7. Live Must-Know sessions will be hosted regularly so you can always find one to fit your schedule

  8. The timings will be appropriate for the language and feature many time zones

  9. They are included in your SAP Enterprise Support maintenance agreement

  10. These interactive sessions are SAP Enterprise Support’s newest training option


SAP S/4HANA is THE Intelligent Suite for the digital economy, can you afford to miss this essential starter pack in your journey to become an intelligent enterprise?



Join our live interactive sessions to gain valuable insight!


Before you can access the links below, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. 





  • Getting started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals




  • SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check




  • SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion




  • Custom Code Adaptation for SAP S/4HANA



  • Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA Finance

    What about an online collaboration platform?


    Would you like more? Why not take a look at this excellent blog that will tell you how to get started with our online collaboration platform: The SAP Enterprise Support value maps, and show you the many topics and other features that our value maps boast.


    Note: To register for the value map, you may require access to SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. Already-registered members can access the value map directly via the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps learning room

entry.title: Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals
entry.content:
If you need to pin down the differences between SAP S/4HANA and SAP ERP and how to plan your transition, or if you are thinking of migrating your business to the cloud or even a complete landscape transformation, you will find all the fundamental information to get you started and safely on your way with this 105 minute, interactive live must-know webcast.

If you need just one 105 minute session to help your SAP S/4HANA project get off the ground, then this is it - Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals.

During this live must-know session on Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals, you will:

  • Get an overview of SAP S/4HANA and how it compares to SAP ERP.

  • Find out about the 2 possible deployment options available to you.

  • Investigate the 3 different transition paths you can take.

  • Explore the SAP Activate methodology

  • Receive an introduction to SAP Model Company.

  • Learn what are the 2 SAP S/4HANA trial options

  • See how you can take advantage of the simplified data model to accelerate and streamline your business processes.

  • Experience live demonstrations of:

    • The powerful SAP S/4HANA User Interface -SAP Fiori.

    • SAP S/4HANA embedded analytics

    • The SAP roadmap viewer

    • SAP Best Practice Explorer




And as if that wasn't enough, you will also get some insight into the SAP Enterprise Support guides program, the relevant SAP Enterprise Support value maps and the Enterprise Support Academy.

These Live Must-Know interactive webcasts are available in many languages, check the list below for your local language.
Before you can access the links below, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

Register now - Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals 


English (APJ & NA), German, Spanish, Portuguese, French, Chinese, Japanese and Arabic

 

What are Live Must-Know interactive webcasts? Read our blog to find out more.

Other Interactive Sessions.…


  • Understanding SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA




  • SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion




  • Custom Code Adaptation for SAP S/4HANA




  • Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA Finance





Join our online collaboration platform?

Why not take a look at this excellent blog that will tell you how to get started with our online collaboration platform: The SAP Enterprise Support value maps, and show you the many topics and other features that our value maps boast.
Note: To register for the value map, you may require access to SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. Already-registered members can access the value map directly via the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps learning room

Register for the SAP S/4HANA value map

entry.title: Interactive training for SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion
entry.content:
If you are planning to convert your SAP ECC system to SAP S/4HANA, one of the first critical activities is to convert your sandbox system. Very often, the workload for the first sandbox conversion is underestimated, which can lead to overruns in time and budget.

The live must-know interactive training for SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion will assist you in this critical activity and highlight the must-know facts to get you started and well on your way for your first sandbox conversion.

This 105-minute interactive training complements our 3-part curriculum of recorded meet-the-expert webcasts (see links below) which takes a much deeper look at the topics but requires a considerably greater time investment, hence, the choice of Live Must-Know webcast or MTE is dependent on time and detail required. This interactive session is available monthly in English German and Chinese.
Before you can access the links below, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.

Register now : SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion
Monthly in English, German and Chinese.


 

During this 100 minute interactive session, we will:

  • Introduce the topic ​

    • Role(s)/of a sandbox in a conversion project /Why/When ​



  • Discuss planning your sandbox conversion​

    • Functional prerequisites​

    • Technical prerequisites​

    • Making the right choice, Understanding SUM , DMO of SUM, DMO with system move or ascsmove​

    • Understanding the impact of shadow instance on target in 1909 and above​



  • Guide you on how to execute the sandbox conversion​

    • Overview of the execution of SUM : Uptime​

    • Overview of the execution of SUM : Downtime​



  • Explore how to analyze and improve Downtime (if time allows)​

    • Analysis of upganafiles​

    • Advanced approaches​



  • Take a look at lessons learnt from SAP S/4HANA projects


 

What are Live Must-Know interactive webcasts? Read our blog to find out more.

Other Interactive Sessions.…


  • Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals




  • Understanding SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA




  • Custom Code Adaptation for SAP S/4HANA




  • Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA Finance




3-Part Sandbox Conversion MTE curriculum

  • Part 1: Prerequisites

  • Part 2: Maintenance Planner

  • Part 3a: DMO of SUMfor SAP ECC on AnyDB

  • Part 3b: SUMfor SAP ECC on SAP HANA


Other Useful Resources

  • DMO Guide

  • SUM 2.0 Guide

  • Maintenance Planner Help Portal

  • Installation Guide for SAP S/4HANA


 

Join our online collaboration platform

Why not take a look at this excellent blog that will tell you how to get started with our online collaboration platform: The SAP Enterprise Support value maps.
Note: To register for the value map, you may require access to SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support. Already-registered members can access the value map directly via the SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps learning room

Register for the SAP S/4HANA value map

entry.title: Getting started with Identity and Access management (IAM) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
• Do you want to gain an insight into Identity and Access management in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition?

  • Are you curious about learning how to effectively manage IAM functions and processes in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition?

  • Are you interested in understanding project planning guidelines and how to manage roles/security across quality and production systems in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition?

  • Do you want to learn about the key fundamental authorization concepts and SAP best practices for IAM in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition?


If the answer is yes to any of the above, then please make sure to register for the upcoming two-part empowerment session with our experts.
* Before you can access the above links, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by step guide to registration can be found here.
(Recordings will be made available via the above links)

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Identity and Access Management Getting Started (Part 1)
March 1st, 2021 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CET/Berlin*

SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Identity and Access Management: Implementation Methodology (Part 2)
March 5th, 2021 4:00 PM - 5:30 PM CET/Berlin*

During these live empowerment sessions, you will:



  • Discover business roles and catalogs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition.

  • Explore the tools available to help support IAM functions and processes.

  • Identify SAP best practices for IAM in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, essentials edition.

  • Learn the project planning guidelines for IAM security design.

  • Understand how to manage roles and security across quality and production systems.

  • Gain an insight into the IAM accelerators available, to quickly get started with IAM.

  • Increase proficiency in the key fundamental authorization concepts, such as the naming convention, the definition and function of the workplace and business roles.

entry.title: Gain the skills needed for your journey with SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Are you short on time and want a training session that is packed with only the essential facts on a topic?

We have the answer for you! Our new live sessions named "live must-know" offered by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy. These expert-led sessions are short, but long enough to cover all important facts. We encourage you to interact with our experts and ask questions throughout the session, so you get the most out of your participation.

So, what are Live Must-Know sessions, I hear you ask….

  • They are interactive and full of “must-know” facts

  • They are live, often feature demo’s and there is no recording

  • You are encouraged to ask questions throughout the sessions with engineered pauses

  • They are long enough to give you the facts but short enough to be cost-effective with time (~90 mins)

  • You will get the presentation slides full of useful tips and additional resources

  • Some of these sessions are hosted in several languages– visit the abstracts for language details

  • Live Must-Know sessions will be hosted regularly so you can always find one to fit your schedule

  • They are included in your SAP Enterprise Support maintenance agreement at no additional costs. All that is required is your s-user ID and a registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support



Join our sessions to gain valuable insight and the build the skills needed!


We currently offer 5 hot topics around SAP S/4HANA. Pick the topics most relevant to you, select your preferred language and time zone and make yourself ready to take the next step on your SAP S/4HANA conversion.

Before you can access the links below, a one-time registration in SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. 


  • Getting started with SAP S/4HANA: Fundamentals




  • SAP S/4HANA Readiness Check




  • SAP S/4HANA Sandbox Conversion




  • Custom Code Adaptation for SAP S/4HANA




  • Getting Started with SAP S/4HANA Finance




 


Connect to our experts through our online collaboration platform

Why not take a look at this excellent video that will tell you how to get started with our online collaboration platform: The SAP Enterprise Support value maps.

Sign up today

entry.title: SAP Screen Personas roadmap update December 2021 - moving to the cloud
entry.content:
As we look forward to 2022, it’s time to update the roadmap for SAP Screen Personas. Our goal is to help you plan your UX transformation projects along with any other SAP-related upgrades that might be on your list. As always, we prioritize our investment decisions based on customer impact, so your feedback drives our innovations. We greatly value the ongoing dialogue with our customers and partners, as it presents an opportunity to learn how you are using the product.

SAP Screen Personas is closely tied to SAP S/4HANA

Organizationally, the SAP Screen Personas development team is still part of SAP UX Engineering. This allows us to take advantage of the synergies with SAP Fiori elements and SAP Fiori tools, since all three of these products are aimed at improving the user experience for SAP’s ERP customers. We roll into SAP S/4HANA, which makes sense, since we bring the value of personalization and simplification to our customers. Collaboration with our colleagues across the SAP S/4HANA organization leads us to our latest (planned*) innovation.


 

Adapt the UI for classic applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud

For the past decade, SAP’s ERP customers could improve their user experience using SAP Screen Personas. Soon, we are planning to make SAP Screen Personas available to simplify classic applications in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, which is our public cloud version of our flagship ERP system. “Classic applications” refers to the SAP GUI screens that do not yet have a corresponding SAP Fiori app based on SAPUI5.

Specifically, we are planning to make the following capabilities available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud:

  1. Flavor editor

  2. Script editor

  3. Template editor

  4. The ability for authorized users to select flavors


There are a few key differences between the SAP Screen Personas add-on and the version that will be part of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • SAP Screen Personas will be pre-installed into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, unlike the current add-on that you install into your ECC or SAP S/4HANA system.

  • Theming will come from SAP S/4HANA Cloud using the UI Theme Designer, so we will not include the SAP Screen Personas theme editor in the version for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

  • Since the personalization capabilities will be a core part of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud offering, we will not include the SAP Screen Personas branding. Access to the personalization capabilities will be through the “Adapt UI” feature (same wording used for SAPUI5 flexibility that allows you to move fields around in SAP Fiori apps) rather than the familiar blue “P” pulldown at the top of the screen.

  • The admin functions will be adjusted, since this is not a separate add-on, but integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud identity and access management. You will still assign flavors to roles in the system, but in a different way.


For SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud, since you control the complete environment, you install the SAP Screen Personas add-on, similar to how you would in an on-premise system. This means that screen personalization and simplification capabilities are available to anyone that wants them, regardless of how they choose to move to the cloud (or stay on-prem).

We are working on a new openSAP course on how to “Adapt the UI for classic applications in SAP S/4HANA.” It will include all the latest SAP Screen Personas functionality for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. (We also plan to include units for on-prem SAP S/4HANA and ECC systems). We are planning to release the course in mid-2022.

SAP Screen Personas 3.0 continues to evolve

With all the work to bring SAP Screen Personas into SAP S/4HANA Cloud, we still made time to add new functionality for our existing customers. In addition to planning for the release of SP14 later this year, we have several other innovations listed on our roadmap. We will continue to make SAP Screen Personas easier to use.

We position SAP Screen Personas as an easy way to simplify screens to improve usability, which it is. However, there is always room for improvement. We’re working on providing more documentation, tutorials, and how-to videos so you can make the best use of the product.

Aligning with the evolving SAP Fiori design system is another development area. We will be adding support for the Horizon theme to SAP Screen Personas when Horizon becomes available in SAP S/4HANA.

Longer term, we, like almost every other part of SAP, are looking at how to integrate machine learning and artificial intelligence to do more of the work for you when creating flavors.

View the latest official SAP Screen Personas roadmap at https://roadmaps.sap.com/. Search for SAP Screen Personas in the SAP Roadmap Explorer.

How you can influence the roadmap

  • Tell us what you want to see in future versions. It can be ideas for new features or a story about what you want the product to do that it cannot today. Leave us a comment below or send us an email.

  • Attend the monthly SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum to see how other customers use SAP Screen Personas to improve employee productivity. There is generally a customer demo each month, SAP tutorial, and discussion about technical topics. Register for the SAP Screen Personas practitioner forum.


Where to learn more

  • Take one of our openSAP courses. By taking the introduction, advanced, and mobile courses, many customers have renovated their SAP user experience without any additional assistance from us.

  • Visit the SAP help portal for SAP Screen Personas, which includes all our documentation in a convenient, searchable format.


For the SAP UX Engineering team, peter.spielvogel.

 

Disclaimer:

* This blog contains forward-looking statements about SAP‘s strategy and possible future developments. This information is subject to change and may be changed by SAP at any time for any reason without notice.

entry.title: New Feature in the SAP Road Map Explorer: Product Vision Column
entry.content:
What is changing?

  • Kanban board
    • Every RM item > 4 quarters into the future will be combined in one product vision column, instead of showing several separate vision columns
    • No change in the set-up of “current quarter” plus the 4 next of planned innovations
    • Time horizon button, in which we have now 2 options:

(1) choice of a variable range – use the circle to drag and define the range (chose earliest, current, latest, as until now)

(2) choice of a specific range – use to define a specific quarter(s) range, as today.

  • Solution capability view at innovation level
    • The same logic as above is applied here as well.

Have a look at https://roadmaps.sap.com/welcome.

entry.title: Top Picks: Innovations Highlights from SAP Business Technology Platform (Q4/2023)
entry.content:
The innovation highlights blog for SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform) is back in 2024. I´m excited to see which innovations are coming up in 2024 for SAP BTP.  But isn´t it worthwhile reviewing the top Q4 2023 highlights of our recent deliveries out of the SAP BTP product roadmap as well? 

Join me to take a closer look at a subset of my personal innovation highlights of SAP BTP in Q4 2023 and an outlook of what´s coming up next.

SAP BTP Innovation Highlights Q4 2023

Application Development and Automation:

  • With Q4 2023, SAP Build Apps is now available with the SAP BTP Trial Account. Trial Accounts allow to experience and evaluate the key functions of SAP Build Apps, the low-code application development tool. If you ready to experience SAP Build Apps then activate your Trial Account right now following a few simple steps. Learn how to get started with the blog post of @EsmeeX.
  • SAP Build Work Zone got some new features and enhancements as well. A new site experience for SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition, import and export of site menu for the advanced edition are just two new features for a smooth admin experience. Business users can be happy as well as they also got a new improvement for the guided experience with wizard layout. Check out also the integration of Joule, our digital assistant, in SAP Start. Read the blog post of @Vera_Gutbrod to get all updates.
  • In Q4 2023 SAP Build Process Automation released a new set of 19 content packages - 12 business content and 7 learning content crafted for IT, Sales, Services, Sourcing, Finance, and Procurement. A detailed list on the new content can be found here. Check out also the blog post of @JesseSukhbayar to get more insights on what´s new with SAP Build Process Automation. If you have found interesting content and need to know how to deploy the pre-built content in SAP Build Process Automation I can highly recommend to watch the video demonstrating it. Learn how easy it is and configure step by step to fine-tune the content before deploying it in your project. 

Integration:

  • Concentrating on our flagship integration product, SAP Integration Suite, we released a flexible hybrid integration runtime: Edge Integration Cell. The new extension of SAP Integration Suite as Kubernetes based solution provides the option of a local, customer-managed environment of the SAP Integration Suite. With this we provide customers with hybrid or private environment the flexibility to choose the runtime for the execution of ground-to-ground or hybrid integration scenarios depending on their use cases. Edge Integration Cell supports for example the execution of integrations between local on premise systems without any data leaving the local datacenter and without loosing the comfort of having a central design and management solution for all integration scenarios. It helps a lot of customers in certain industries to meet region specific laws and regulations. Check out the announcement blog post of @piyush_gakhar and the FAQ blog post of @finny_babu to get more insights. For 2024 there are already several innovations on our Road Map Explorer for Edge Integration Cell. 
  • To provide enhanced B2B integration, Trading Partner Management capability enables now support for validating and converting the Tradacoms message format by introducing a new flow step. It provides the full end-to-end support for Tradacoms messages in Trading Partner Management and you are able to use the type system for modelling guidelines in Integration Advisor. Check out the blogpost of  @joerg_ackermann for more information.

Data and Analytics:

  • The data lake realational engine in SAP HANA Cloud has with Q4 2023 the ability to read and write data from nearly any files instance of SAP HANA Cloud data lake. This significantly improves the integration between instances of SAP HANA Cloud and broaden access to data sources from the relational engine of the data lake for SAP HANA Cloud. You will benefit from expanded access to diverse data sources, fostering improved data utilization and reinforcing the synergy between various SAP HANA Cloud instances. If you want to learn more check out the blog post of @thomashammer to get more highlights on what´s new for SAP HANA Cloud in December. 
  • Additionally with Q4 2023 you have new options of data center availability and hyperscaler options for SAP Analytics Cloud through the deployment of Google Cloud Platform (GCP) in the Asia and Europe region.  

Artificial Intelligence:

  • At TechEd 2023 in November we did not only announce the generative AI hub - it also got released just a few weeks later. The generative AI hub provides instant access to a broad range of large language models (LLMs) from different providers and partners like GPT-4 by Azure OpenAI or OpenSource Falcon-40b. With this access, developers can orchestrate multiple models, whether programmatically via SAP AI Core or via the playground within SAP AI Launchpad. Check out the blog post of @HadiHares. 
  • With the Q4 2023 release the new Document Information Extraction, premium edition supercharged by AI capabilities, makes the process of different types of documents without increased costs or more manual work possible. It automates all business document use cases in most languages end to end, provides built-in support for 40+ languages with extensions for virtually any kind of document and makes extensions of exisiting extraction routines possible. To get more information read the blog post of @tomasz_janasz.
  • If you are interested in a deep-dive of SAP´s Generative AI strategy make sure you read the blog post of @philipp_herzig. The blog post is the first of a series that dives deeper into various aspects of SAP’s approach to generative AI and its technical underpinnings. 

Cross SAP BTP:

  • Guidance on the best way to develop extensions and applications on SAP BTP is a topic everyone is lacking for. Our in Q4 2023 released SAP BTP Guidance Framework is the compass for architecting, implementing, and operating solutions on SAP BTP. It includes elements like decision guides, reference architectures, methodologies, recommendations and DevOps. One resource within the SAP BTP Guidance Framework which was also released in the last quarter of 2023 is the SAP BTP Developer´s Guide. The guide helps on how to implement business applications on SAP BTP and explains the building blocks for developing, delivering, and integrating business applications.
  • Our famous SAP Discovery Center also got some updates. In addition to missions and services the SAP Discovery Center cosmos now includes reference architectures. As of now you can find integration, data and analytics and hyperscaler references architectures in our catalog. Each reference architecture provides you guiding information which helps to understand its scope, mechanics, how it relates to SAP business applications and guidance when to choose a particular reference architecture. The SAP BTP reference architectures are also part of the just mentioned SAP BTP Guidance Framework. 
  • With Q4 2023 we also announced the availability of the SAP Application Extension Methodology version 2.2. The SAP Application Extension Methodology supports and guides you by providing a structured, technology agnostic approach to define an organization specific extension strategy. Version 2.2 offers now a full documentation on the SAP Help Portal, improved technology agnostic extension tasks and a refined extension technology mapping. Check out the blog post of @MarcoMatha to learn more.

SAP BTP Innovation Highlights - outlook:

 Please find here my personal innovation highlights outlook coming up:

  • Application Development and Automation: SAP Build Code
  • Application Development and Automation: Generating a business service of ABAP RESTful application programming model
  • Integration: AdvancedEventMesh adapter for event mediation
  • Integration: Support for custom SOA messages in Integration Advisor
  • Data and Analytics: Next-generation natural language query (NLQ) powered by AI for SAP Analytics Cloud
  • Data and Analytics: Support for storage and retrieval of vector embeddings in SAP HANA Cloud
  • SAP Identity and Access Management: Identity Authentication service integration with SAP Cloud ALM

Check out all upcoming innovations in our SAP Road Map Explorer to learn more.

Check out more on innovation highlights:

  • Follow the Roadmap Explorer, which is updated on a regular basis to always stay on top of the latest innovations 🔊
  • Subscribe to our monthly newsletter to get all SAP BTP product news 📫
  • Check out the next blog post beginning of Q2/2024 summarizing the highlights of the previous quarter 🗒
  • If you want to be up to date on the latest SAP BTP news and innovations check out the new monthly SAP Innobytes video series 📹
  • Want to get a recap on what´s new in SAP BTP then check out the What´s New in SAP BTP quarterly series
  • Mark your calendar for the latest SAP events coming up 📅

👉 Read other SAP Business Technology Platform blog posts and check the SAP Business Technology Platform Topic Page where you can also ask questions. Feel free to follow my profile stephanie.stang that you do not miss my next personal view of innovation highlights for SAP BTP for Q1 2024.
